response:
# Multi-Page Government Benefits Portal Accessibility Audit Plan

> **For Claude:** Use a11y-planner protocol. Review with a11y-critic at each audit checkpoint.
> **Compliance target:** WCAG 2.2 AA, Section 508, VPAT 2.4 Rev 508
> **Users who need accessibility:** Citizens using screen readers, keyboard-only navigation, screen magnification, mobile assistive tech, voice input, high contrast mode, reduced motion, and cognitive supports
> **Assistive technologies:** NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver macOS, iOS VoiceOver, TalkBack Android, keyboard-only, Dragon NaturallySpeaking

**Feature:** Full accessibility audit plan for a 20-page government benefits portal preparing a VPAT in 3 months.  
**Risk Level:** High  
**Component/Page Type:** Site-level audit covering public pages, authenticated flows, shared interactive components, third-party integrations, and VPAT reporting.

---

## Scope & Context

This is not a component implementation plan. It is an audit execution plan for a live government benefits portal with existing code, known incremental fixes, and no prior systematic audit. The output must support a defensible VPAT 2.4 Rev 508 publication.

The audit should follow **WCAG-EM: Website Accessibility Conformance Evaluation Methodology** as the sampling and reporting framework. The site has 20 pages, but equal-depth testing of every page is the wrong model. The audit must prioritize:

1. **Critical citizen task flows**: account creation, login, multi-step application, document upload, status dashboard, notification center.
2. **Shared components**: global navigation, mega-menu, search, alerts, data tables, file upload, date picker, timeout modal.
3. **Representative public content templates**: home, listings, detail variants, FAQ, news, contact.
4. **Third-party integrations**: reCAPTCHA v2, embedded YouTube, analytics.

The QA team has two engineers and three months. The plan therefore uses a **risk-weighted sampling model** with shared-component leverage. A defect in the mega-menu may affect all 20 pages; a defect in one program detail template may affect only that template family.

What this plan is **not** claiming: it does not guarantee full legal conformance by testing a sample alone. It defines a practical, WCAG-EM-aligned audit approach for identifying, remediating, retesting, and documenting conformance claims and exceptions for VPAT publication.

---

## Audit Methodology

Use WCAG-EM phases:

1. **Define scope**: benefits portal, authenticated and public pages, WCAG 2.2 AA, Section 508.
2. **Explore site**: identify templates, user journeys, shared components, states, and third-party content.
3. **Select representative sample**: include all critical flows, all shared components, all high-risk templates, and at least one page per content type.
4. **Audit selected sample**: automated, manual keyboard, screen reader, visual, focus, ARIA, mobile, and documentation review.
5. **Report findings**: issue severity, WCAG criterion, affected templates/components, remediation owner, retest status.
6. **Prepare VPAT claim**: conformance level per criterion, supporting evidence, known exceptions, third-party limitations.

WCAG-EM reference: https://www.w3.org/WAI/test-evaluate/conformance/wcag-em/

---

## Page Sampling Strategy

### Priority 1: Critical Authenticated Journeys

These receive full-depth testing because failures block access to benefits.

| Page / Flow | Why It Is High Risk | Required Test Depth |
|---|---|---|
| Account creation | First access point; forms, errors, passwords, validation | Full |
| Login | Authentication, reCAPTCHA, errors, session handling | Full |
| Multi-step application step 1 | Start of primary benefits task | Full |
| Multi-step application steps 2-5 | Repeated form patterns, conditional fields, redundant entry risk | Representative plus branch testing |
| Multi-step application step 6 review/submit | Legal confirmation, error prevention, final submission | Full |
| Document upload | File input, status, errors, progress, accepted formats | Full |
| Application status dashboard | Tables, status badges, links, alerts | Full |
| Notification center | Lists, read/unread state, pagination or filters | Full |
| Session timeout warning modal | Time limit, modal focus trap, extension action | Full |

WCAG emphasis: 2.1.1 Keyboard, 2.1.2 No Keyboard Trap, 2.2.1 Timing Adjustable, 2.4.3 Focus Order, 2.4.7 Focus Visible, 2.4.11 Focus Not Obscured, 3.3.1 Error Identification, 3.3.2 Labels or Instructions, 3.3.7 Redundant Entry, 3.3.8 Accessible Authentication, 4.1.2 Name Role Value, 4.1.3 Status Messages.

### Priority 2: Shared Components

Test each shared component in isolation and in at least two page contexts.

| Component | Primary Risks | Pages To Include |
|---|---|---|
| Global navigation with mega-menu | Keyboard access, Escape behavior, focus restoration, hover-only content | Home, authenticated dashboard |
| Search with live results | Live announcements, keyboard interaction, result count, focus stability | Home, program listings |
| Alerts / notifications | Status messages, dismiss buttons, urgency | Dashboard, application form |
| Data tables | Headers, row/column relationships, sorting, status text | Application history |
| File upload | Native input access, drag alternative, progress/errors | Document upload |
| Date picker | Keyboard model, input fallback, format instructions | Application form |
| Session timeout modal | Focus trap, Escape, timing extension, return focus | Authenticated flow |
| In-page alerts | Non-color indicators, live regions, close buttons | Contact, application flow |

### Priority 3: Public Content Templates

Test representative pages at medium depth, escalating if defects indicate template-level risk.

| Page | Test Depth | Rationale |
|---|---:|---|
| Home | Medium | Shared nav, search, alerts, content landmarks |
| About | Light | Static content, headings, links |
| Program Listings | Medium | Search, filters, lists, cards |
| Program Detail variant 1 | Medium | Embedded YouTube, content structure |
| Program Detail variant 2 | Medium | Template variation |
| Program Detail variant 3 | Medium | Template variation |
| FAQ | Medium | Disclosure pattern risk |
| News | Light | Article/list structure |
| Contact | Full | Form, validation, reCAPTCHA |

### Execution Order

1. Inventory templates, states, and shared components.
2. Run automated baseline scans across all 20 pages.
3. Fully test account creation, login, application flow, document upload.
4. Test shared components and confirm affected page coverage.
5. Test dashboard and notification center.
6. Test public content templates.
7. Test third-party integrations and document limitations.
8. Retest remediated critical and major issues.
9. Draft VPAT.
10. Run a11y-critic review on findings, remediation evidence, and VPAT claims.

---

## Tool Selection Rationale

| Tool / Method | Use | Why |
|---|---|---|
| axe DevTools Enterprise | Primary automated rules engine | Licensed, repeatable, CI-compatible, strong WCAG rule coverage |
| Playwright + axe-core | Regression and state testing | Tests rendered states, auth flows, expanded menus, modals, error states |
| WAVE | Manual visual overlay support | Useful for human review of structure, labels, regions, contrast clues |
| Lighthouse | Supplemental only | Good broad signal, not sufficient for audit evidence |
| Manual keyboard testing | Required | Only reliable way to validate focus order, traps, Escape, composite widgets |
| Screen reader testing | Required | Validates names, roles, states, live regions, reading order |
| Visual regression | Required for focus/zoom | Confirms focus visibility, reflow, high contrast, reduced motion |
| DOM verification | Required | Confirms ARIA attributes land on the correct rendered elements |

Axe, WAVE, and Lighthouse are not substitutes for each other. Axe provides repeatable rule detection; WAVE helps visual inspection; Lighthouse is a quick supplemental scan. None can prove keyboard operability or screen reader announcement quality.

---

## Automated Testing Setup

### Axe DevTools Enterprise

Configure scans for:

- WCAG 2.2 AA rules where available.
- Section 508 relevant rules.
- All public page templates.
- Authenticated pages using test accounts.
- Component states: default, expanded, collapsed, loading, error, submitted, disabled, timeout warning.

Do not exclude third-party widgets globally. Instead:

- Scan pages with third-party content included.
- Tag findings as first-party or third-party.
- Document third-party findings separately for VPAT exceptions.
- Avoid suppressions unless the ownership and rationale are recorded.

### Playwright + axe-core

Use Playwright for repeatable regression checks on:

- Login default, error, and reCAPTCHA-present states.
- Multi-step application form default, validation error, save, review, submit states.
- Mega-menu closed/open states.
- Search loading/results/no-results states.
- File upload idle/progress/error/success states.
- Date picker closed/open/selected/error states.
- Timeout modal open/extend/logout states.
- Dashboard table default/sorted/empty states.

DOM verification must confirm:

- `aria-expanded` on the trigger, not the panel.
- `aria-controls` references an existing `id`.
- `aria-invalid` is on the invalid input.
- `aria-describedby` references visible or programmatically available help/error text.
- `role="status"` or `aria-live` is not repeated inside loops.
- `aria-modal="true"` is present only on true modal dialogs.
- Hidden inactive content is not tabbable.

WCAG: 1.3.1, 2.1.1, 2.4.3, 3.3.1, 4.1.2, 4.1.3.

---

## Interaction Pattern Design

### Interactive Elements Table

| Widget | APG Pattern | Keyboard Model | ARIA Attributes | WCAG Citation |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Mega-menu | APG Disclosure or Navigation Menu Button, depending implementation: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/disclosure/ | Tab to top-level controls; Enter/Space opens; Escape closes; Tab moves through links | `aria-expanded`, `aria-controls`, optional `aria-current` | 2.1.1, 2.4.3, 4.1.2 |
| Search live results | APG Combobox if suggestions are selectable: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/combobox/ | Type in input; Down/Up through suggestions if present; Enter selects/submits; Escape clears/closes | `role="combobox"` only if behavior matches; `aria-expanded`, `aria-controls`, `aria-activedescendant`, `aria-live` result count | 2.1.1, 4.1.2, 4.1.3 |
| FAQ items | APG Disclosure: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/disclosure/ | Tab to question button; Enter/Space toggles | `aria-expanded`, `aria-controls` | 2.1.1, 4.1.2 |
| Session timeout | APG Modal Dialog: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/dialog-modal/ | Focus enters dialog; Tab trapped; Escape behavior defined; Enter activates primary action | `role="dialog"`, `aria-modal`, `aria-labelledby`, `aria-describedby` | 2.1.2, 2.2.1, 2.4.3, 4.1.2 |
| File upload | Native file input plus status region | Tab to file input/button; Space/Enter opens picker; no drag-only operation | `aria-describedby`, `aria-invalid`, `aria-busy`, `role="status"` | 2.1.1, 2.5.7, 3.3.2, 4.1.3 |
| Date picker | APG Date Picker Dialog if custom: https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/dialog-modal/examples/datepicker-dialog/ | Text input fallback; button opens calendar; arrows move dates; Enter selects; Escape closes | `aria-haspopup`, `aria-expanded`, dialog attributes, grid roles if custom calendar | 2.1.1, 2.4.3, 3.3.2, 4.1.2 |
| Data table sorting | APG sortable table guidance | Tab to sortable header buttons; Enter/Space changes sort | `aria-sort` on active sorted header | 1.3.1, 2.1.1, 4.1.2 |
| Alerts | Status / Alert pattern | Dismiss button keyboard operable if present | `role="status"` for nonurgent, `role="alert"` for urgent; `aria-describedby` where linked | 4.1.3 |
| Notification center | Native list or table, optional disclosure/filter patterns | Tab through controls; Enter opens notification; state must be clear | `aria-current`, `aria-pressed`, `aria-label`, or text state as appropriate | 1.3.1, 2.4.4, 4.1.2 |
| reCAPTCHA v2 | Third-party widget | Must be keyboard reachable and have accessible fallback | Document third-party support/limitations | 3.3.8, 4.1.2 |
| YouTube embed | Media player | Keyboard operable controls; captions available | iframe title; captions/transcript links | 1.2.2, 1.2.3, 2.1.1, 4.1.2 |

---

## Focus Management Verification

### Global Focus Requirements

- First focusable element should be “Skip to main content.” WCAG 2.4.1.
- Focus order must follow visual and logical order. WCAG 2.4.3.
- Focus indicators must be visible and not obscured by sticky headers or banners. WCAG 2.4.7, 2.4.11, 2.4.13.
- Custom focus styling must meet 3:1 contrast and should use a two-color indicator. WCAG 1.4.11, 2.4.13.
- No keyboard trap unless inside a modal, and modal trap must provide exit. WCAG 2.1.2.

### Component-Specific Focus Checks

| Component | Focus Checks |
|---|---|
| Mega-menu | Focus opens or button opens menu predictably; Escape closes; focus returns to trigger; no hidden links in tab order |
| Search results | Typing does not steal focus; suggestion navigation is arrow-key based if combobox; Escape closes suggestions |
| Multi-step form | On next step, focus moves to step heading or first invalid field depending outcome |
| Validation errors | On submit failure, focus moves to error summary; summary links move focus to invalid fields |
| File upload | Upload progress does not steal focus; success/error announced; retry/remove controls reachable |
| Date picker | Opening moves focus into calendar dialog only if custom picker; closing returns focus to trigger/input |
| Timeout modal | Focus moves to modal; trapped; returns to prior focused control after extend; logout path clear |
| Dashboard table | Sorting does not reset focus unexpectedly; sorted header retains focus |
| Notification center | Delete/read actions preserve focus after list updates |

For list CRUD behavior: after deletion, focus moves to the next item at the same index, or previous/empty-state control if none remains. WCAG 2.4.3.

---

## State Communication Design

### State Communication Table

| State | Visual Indicator | Programmatic Indicator | ARIA / Semantic Attribute | WCAG Citation |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Navigation menu expanded/collapsed | Panel visible/hidden, chevron | Trigger state | `aria-expanded`, `aria-controls` | 4.1.2 |
| Current page | Active styling and text/icon | Current location | `aria-current="page"` | 2.4.4, 4.1.2 |
| Search loading | Spinner plus “Searching” text | Status message | `aria-busy`, `role="status"` | 4.1.3 |
| Search results count | Count text | Polite announcement | `aria-live="polite"` or `role="status"` | 4.1.3 |
| Form invalid | Error text, icon, border | Field invalid and described | `aria-invalid`, `aria-describedby` | 1.3.1, 3.3.1, 4.1.2 |
| Disabled control | Disabled appearance plus text reason where needed | Native disabled or ARIA disabled | `disabled` or `aria-disabled` | 4.1.2 |
| Required field | Visible “required” text or marker plus legend | Programmatic required | `required` or `aria-required` | 1.3.1, 3.3.2, 4.1.2 |
| File upload progress | Progress bar and percent text | Status/progress semantics | Native progress, `aria-valuenow`, `role="status"` | 1.3.1, 4.1.3 |
| Upload error | Error text and icon | Error associated to input | `aria-invalid`, `aria-describedby` | 3.3.1, 4.1.2 |
| Table sorted | Sort icon plus text | Sort state | `aria-sort` | 1.3.1, 4.1.2 |
| Notification unread | Bold text plus “Unread” label | Text state, not color only | Visible text or accessible name | 1.4.1, 4.1.2 |
| Timeout warning | Modal, countdown text | Dialog and live updates | `role="dialog"`, `aria-modal`, cautious live region | 2.2.1, 4.1.3 |
| CAPTCHA challenge | Widget state | Third-party semantics | Document observed support and alternatives | 3.3.8, 4.1.2 |

Use one announcement region per event class. Do not place assertive live regions in repeated field templates. Field errors should be connected with `aria-describedby`; summary-level submit failure may use a single alert/status region.

Visual symbols such as `+`, `-`, `×`, `▼`, or warning icons must be hidden from assistive tech when redundant with text or ARIA state. WCAG 1.1.1, 4.1.2.

---

## Visual Accessibility Plan

Test every sampled page and shared component for:

- Normal text contrast at least 4.5:1. WCAG 1.4.3.
- Large text contrast at least 3:1. WCAG 1.4.3.
- Non-text component contrast at least 3:1. WCAG 1.4.11.
- Focus indicator contrast and area meeting WCAG 2.4.7 and 2.4.13.
- Color not used as sole indicator. WCAG 1.4.1.
- Body links distinguishable by more than color, preferably underline. WCAG 1.4.1.
- Text resize to 200% without loss. WCAG 1.4.4.
- Reflow at 320 CSS px equivalent without horizontal scrolling except true data tables. WCAG 1.4.10.
- Touch targets at least 24x24 CSS px minimum, 44x44 preferred. WCAG 2.5.8.
- Reduced motion respected for transitions, loading, and scrolling. WCAG 2.3.3.
- High contrast / forced colors mode remains usable.

Do not rely on screenshots alone. Confirm the DOM and computed styles where issues are subtle, especially focus and hidden content.

---

## Content Accessibility Plan

### Public Pages

- Landmarks must identify navigation, main content, search, footer. WCAG 1.3.1, 2.4.1.
- Heading hierarchy must describe page structure without skipping levels for visual styling alone. WCAG 1.3.1, 2.4.6.
- Link text must describe purpose without relying on surrounding visual layout. WCAG 2.4.4.
- Program detail pages with YouTube videos need captions and transcripts. WCAG 1.2.2, 1.2.3, 1.2.5.
- Images need appropriate alt text or empty alt when decorative. WCAG 1.1.1.
- FAQ disclosure buttons must use the full question as accessible name. WCAG 2.4.6, 4.1.2.

### Forms

- Every input has a visible label. WCAG 1.3.1, 3.3.2.
- Hints and constraints are associated with fields. WCAG 3.3.2.
- Errors are specific and suggest correction. WCAG 3.3.1, 3.3.3.
- Multi-step form must not re-request previously entered information unless necessary. WCAG 3.3.7.
- Authentication must support paste, password managers, accessible alternatives, and should not rely solely on cognitive puzzles. WCAG 3.3.8.

### Tables

- Use data tables only for tabular data.
- Header cells must use `th` and `scope`.
- Row-identifying cells generated in loops should use `th scope="row"`.
- Never use `role="presentation"` on data tables with semantic headers.
- Sorting state must be visible and programmatic through `aria-sort`.

---

## Third-Party Component Handling

### reCAPTCHA v2

reCAPTCHA is third-party and may create accessibility barriers. The audit must:

- Test keyboard access.
- Test screen reader challenge announcement.
- Test audio challenge availability.
- Test login/contact completion path with NVDA and VoiceOver.
- Document whether an accessible alternative exists.
- Record findings as third-party-controlled unless the implementation wrapper is at fault.

VPAT language should distinguish:

- First-party implementation responsibilities: labels, placement, instructions, error handling, fallback contact path.
- Third-party widget limitations: challenge accessibility, iframe behavior, vendor support.

WCAG focus: 2.1.1, 3.3.8, 4.1.2.

### YouTube Embeds

Audit:

- iframe has descriptive `title`.
- Captions available for all speech.
- Transcript provided near video or linked.
- Player controls keyboard operable.
- Autoplay disabled or pause control available.
- Visual-only information has audio description or text equivalent where needed.

WCAG focus: 1.2.1, 1.2.2, 1.2.3, 1.2.5, 2.1.1, 4.1.2.

### Analytics Script

Audit:

- Script does not inject focusable elements unexpectedly.
- Script does not block zoom, keyboard, or form submission.
- Script does not create unlabeled iframes or controls.
- Privacy banners, if any, are tested as first-class dialogs.

If analytics has no user-facing UI, document as low accessibility risk but verify no DOM interference.

---

## Testing Matrix

| Page / Component | Axe | Playwright State Tests | Keyboard | Screen Reader | Visual / Zoom | VPAT Evidence |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Home | Yes | Nav/search states | Yes | NVDA smoke | Yes | Yes |
| About | Yes | No | Light | No unless issue | Yes | Yes |
| Program Listings | Yes | Search/filter states | Yes | NVDA | Yes | Yes |
| Program Detail variants | Yes | Video embed checks | Yes | VO/NVDA sample | Yes | Yes |
| FAQ | Yes | Disclosure states | Yes | NVDA | Yes | Yes |
| News | Yes | No | Light | No unless issue | Yes | Yes |
| Contact | Yes | Form errors/CAPTCHA | Yes | NVDA + VO | Yes | Yes |
| Account Creation | Yes | All form states | Yes | NVDA + JAWS | Yes | Yes |
| Login | Yes | Error/CAPTCHA/session | Yes | NVDA + JAWS | Yes | Yes |
| Application Form | Yes | Step/error/review states | Yes | NVDA + JAWS | Yes | Yes |
| Document Upload | Yes | Upload states | Yes | NVDA | Yes | Yes |
| Dashboard | Yes | Table/sort/status | Yes | NVDA | Yes | Yes |
| Notification Center | Yes | Read/unread/delete states | Yes | NVDA | Yes | Yes |
| Mega-menu | Yes | Open/close states | Yes | NVDA + VO | Yes | Yes |
| Date picker | Yes | Open/select/error | Yes | NVDA + JAWS | Yes | Yes |
| Timeout modal | Yes | Open/extend/logout | Yes | NVDA + JAWS | Yes | Yes |
| Mobile core flow | Yes where possible | No or limited | Touch/SR gestures | iOS VO + TalkBack | Reflow | Yes |

---

## Screen Reader Methodology

### Matrix

| Assistive Tech | Platform | Primary Coverage |
|---|---|---|
| NVDA | Windows + Chrome/Firefox | Main desktop audit baseline |
| JAWS | Windows + Chrome/Edge | Government/enterprise compatibility check |
| VoiceOver | macOS + Safari | Public pages, media, navigation |
| iOS VoiceOver | iPhone Safari | Login, program listing, application smoke path |
| TalkBack | Android Chrome | Login, dashboard, public content smoke path |
| Dragon NaturallySpeaking | Windows | Voice input labels, button names, form navigation |

### Screen Reader Scenarios

- Navigate landmarks and headings.
- Open and close mega-menu.
- Search and review live results.
- Complete login failure and success paths.
- Complete at least one full application path with errors.
- Upload a document and recover from failed upload.
- Review application status table.
- Read and act on notifications.
- Trigger and recover from timeout warning.
- Play video and find transcript/captions.
- Complete contact form with reCAPTCHA or documented alternative.

Screen reader testing should record actual announcements only where relevant to findings. Avoid filling reports with transcripts of passing behavior.

---

## Prioritization Framework

Severity is based on **user impact and task blockage**, not WCAG criterion number.

| Severity | Definition | Examples | Remediation SLA |
|---|---|---|---|
| CRITICAL | Blocks completion of a core benefits journey with no reasonable workaround | Cannot log in by keyboard; application submit inaccessible; modal traps focus permanently; CAPTCHA blocks SR users | Fix before VPAT publication |
| MAJOR | Significant barrier with workaround or affects many pages/shared components | Mega-menu difficult but bypassable; form errors not associated; table headers missing; search results not announced | Fix before VPAT publication if first-party |
| MINOR | Causes confusion or inefficiency but does not block task completion | Slight heading skip; redundant announcement; inconsistent link text in low-risk content | Fix if capacity allows; document if deferred |
| ADVISORY | Best practice or usability improvement beyond strict failure | Improve helper text, simplify labels, enhance transcript placement | Backlog unless low effort |

Escalate severity if:

- The issue affects a shared component across many pages.
- The issue affects authenticated benefits application flows.
- The issue affects mobile users in a likely high-traffic scenario.
- The issue affects users with no equivalent alternative path.

---

## Acceptance Criteria

The audit is ready to support VPAT drafting when:

- All 20 pages have automated baseline results.
- All critical authenticated flows have full keyboard, screen reader, and visual testing.
- All 8 shared components have state and DOM verification.
- Third-party integrations are tested and documented separately.
- Every finding includes WCAG criterion, severity, affected page/template/component, reproduction steps, expected behavior, actual behavior, owner, and retest status.
- All CRITICAL first-party issues are fixed or explicitly accepted as a launch blocker.
- MAJOR first-party issues are fixed or documented with risk acceptance and remediation timeline.
- VPAT conformance claims are evidence-backed and do not overstate compliance.
- a11y-critic has reviewed the audit methodology, high-risk findings, remediation evidence, and VPAT draft.

---

## Test Cases

- Keyboard: Tab through global navigation, mega-menu, search, form fields, dashboard controls, footer links.
- Keyboard activation: Enter/Space activate buttons, disclosures, submit controls, sort buttons, upload actions.
- Escape: Closes mega-menu, date picker, and timeout modal where applicable.
- Focus trap: Timeout modal traps focus and restores focus after extend/logout.
- Focus visibility: Visible at 100%, 200%, and high contrast mode.
- ARIA state: Verify `aria-expanded`, `aria-selected`, `aria-sort`, `aria-invalid`, `aria-describedby`, `aria-busy`, and live regions in rendered DOM.
- Form validation: Submit empty form, confirm error summary and field-level errors are announced and linked.
- Multi-step flow: Confirm focus movement, step labels, redundant-entry behavior, review/submit error prevention.
- File upload: Test valid file, invalid file type, oversized file, upload failure, success, remove/replace.
- Date picker: Test typed date fallback, calendar keyboard selection, format instructions.
- Screen reader: Confirm landmarks, headings, names, roles, states, and dynamic updates.
- Contrast: Verify normal text, large text, UI boundaries, focus indicators, status colors.
- Reflow: Test 320 CSS px equivalent and 200% zoom without loss of functionality.
- Mobile SR: Complete login and representative application step with iOS VO and TalkBack.
- Third-party: Verify CAPTCHA alternative, YouTube captions/transcripts, analytics no UI interference.

---

## VPAT Reporting Template And Conformance Claim Structure

Use ITI VPAT 2.4 Rev 508.

For each WCAG 2.2 AA criterion, record:

- **Conformance level:** Supports, Partially Supports, Does Not Support, Not Applicable, Not Evaluated.
- **Remarks and explanations:** Plain-language evidence, affected templates/components, known limitations.
- **Ownership:** First-party, third-party, mixed.
- **Evidence source:** axe scan ID, manual test case, screen reader session, remediation ticket, retest result.
- **Status:** Open, remediated, retested, accepted risk, third-party limitation.

Common high-risk VPAT criteria for this portal:

- 1.1.1 Non-text Content
- 1.2.2 Captions
- 1.3.1 Info and Relationships
- 1.3.2 Meaningful Sequence
- 1.4.1 Use of Color
- 1.4.3 Contrast Minimum
- 1.4.10 Reflow
- 1.4.11 Non-text Contrast
- 2.1.1 Keyboard
- 2.1.2 No Keyboard Trap
- 2.2.1 Timing Adjustable
- 2.4.1 Bypass Blocks
- 2.4.3 Focus Order
- 2.4.4 Link Purpose
- 2.4.7 Focus Visible
- 2.4.11 Focus Not Obscured
- 2.5.3 Label in Name
- 2.5.7 Dragging Movements
- 2.5.8 Target Size
- 3.3.1 Error Identification
- 3.3.2 Labels or Instructions
- 3.3.3 Error Suggestion
- 3.3.4 Error Prevention
- 3.3.7 Redundant Entry
- 3.3.8 Accessible Authentication
- 4.1.2 Name Role Value
- 4.1.3 Status Messages

Do not claim “Supports” for a criterion if a critical first-party failure remains in a core journey. For third-party failures, document the limitation, mitigation, and any available alternative path.

---

## Implementation Tasks

### Task 1: Audit Inventory And Sampling Map

🔍 **Review checkpoint after this task**

**Files / Artifacts:**

- Page inventory spreadsheet
- Component inventory
- Third-party integration inventory
- WCAG-EM sampling rationale

**Work:**

- Identify all 20 pages and map them to templates.
- Identify all shared components and where they appear.
- Identify all states for forms, search, upload, date picker, timeout modal.
- Mark first-party vs third-party responsibility.

**Tests:**

- Confirm every page belongs to a template group.
- Confirm every shared component has at least two test contexts.
- Confirm all critical flows are included.

**WCAG Criteria:**

- WCAG-EM methodology support
- Not a criterion-specific test yet

### Task 2: Automated Baseline

🔍 **Review checkpoint after this task**

**Files / Artifacts:**

- Axe DevTools Enterprise project
- Playwright axe scan scripts
- Baseline findings export

**Work:**

- Run axe across all 20 pages.
- Run state scans for high-risk components.
- Tag findings by severity, component, page, and ownership.

**Tests:**

- Default state scan.
- Error state scan.
- Expanded/collapsed state scan.
- Authenticated flow scan.
- DOM placement verification for ARIA attributes.

**WCAG Criteria:**

- 1.1.1, 1.3.1, 1.4.3, 2.4.1, 3.3.2, 4.1.2

### Task 3: Keyboard And Focus Audit

🔍 **Review checkpoint after this task**

**Work:**

- Execute keyboard scripts and manual keyboard passes.
- Validate focus order, traps, restoration, skip links, Escape behavior.
- Test focus not obscured by sticky UI.

**Tests:**

- Tab-only navigation.
- Shift+Tab reverse navigation.
- Enter/Space activation.
- Escape close.
- Modal trap.
- CRUD/list focus after notification actions.
- 200% zoom focus visibility.

**WCAG Criteria:**

- 2.1.1, 2.1.2, 2.4.1, 2.4.3, 2.4.7, 2.4.11, 2.4.13

### Task 4: Screen Reader Audit

🔍 **Review checkpoint after this task**

**Work:**

- Run NVDA baseline on all high-risk flows.
- Run JAWS on login, application, date picker, timeout modal.
- Run VoiceOver on public pages, media, and mobile smoke flows.
- Run TalkBack on mobile login and dashboard smoke paths.

**Tests:**

- Landmark navigation.
- Heading navigation.
- Form label and error announcement.
- Live region behavior.
- Dialog announcement.
- Table navigation.
- CAPTCHA and video behavior.

**WCAG Criteria:**

- 1.3.1, 1.3.2, 2.4.4, 2.4.6, 3.3.1, 3.3.2, 4.1.2, 4.1.3

### Task 5: Visual And Reflow Audit

🔍 **Review checkpoint after this task**

**Work:**

- Test color contrast, focus indicators, reflow, target size, high contrast mode, reduced motion.

**Tests:**

- 100%, 200%, and 400% equivalent checks.
- 320 CSS px equivalent reflow.
- Forced colors mode.
- Reduced motion.
- Touch target measurement.
- Color-only status review.

**WCAG Criteria:**

- 1.4.1, 1.4.3, 1.4.4, 1.4.10, 1.4.11, 2.3.3, 2.5.8

### Task 6: Third-Party Integration Audit

🔍 **Review checkpoint after this task**

**Work:**

- Test reCAPTCHA v2, YouTube, analytics script impact.
- Separate first-party wrapper issues from vendor limitations.
- Draft VPAT remarks for third-party constraints.

**Tests:**

- CAPTCHA keyboard and screen reader path.
- CAPTCHA alternative path.
- YouTube captions/transcripts.
- YouTube keyboard controls.
- Analytics DOM/focus interference.

**WCAG Criteria:**

- 1.2.2, 1.2.3, 1.2.5, 2.1.1, 3.3.8, 4.1.2

### Task 7: Findings Triage And Remediation Tracking

🔍 **Review checkpoint after this task**

**Work:**

- Deduplicate findings by shared component and template.
- Assign CRITICAL, MAJOR, MINOR, ADVISORY.
- Identify remediation leverage: nav, search, alerts, form components, date picker, file upload.

**Tests:**

- Verify severity based on user impact.
- Confirm each finding has reproduction steps and expected behavior.
- Confirm component-level findings list all affected pages.

**WCAG Criteria:**

- All applicable criteria based on finding

### Task 8: Retest And VPAT Evidence Package

🔍 **Review checkpoint after this task**

**Work:**

- Retest fixed critical and major issues.
- Capture before/after evidence.
- Draft VPAT criterion-by-criterion claims.
- Document unresolved first-party and third-party exceptions.

**Tests:**

- Regression checks for remediated components.
- Screen reader retest for high-risk remediations.
- DOM verification after fixes.
- VPAT claim review against evidence.

**WCAG Criteria:**

- All applicable WCAG 2.2 AA criteria

## a11y-Critic Review Checkpoints

| Checkpoint | After Task | Focus Areas |
|---|---|---|
| 🔍 1 | Task 1 | Sampling defensibility, WCAG-EM alignment, missing critical journeys |
| 🔍 2 | Task 2 | Automated scan coverage, false positives/negatives, ARIA DOM verification |
| 🔍 3 | Task 3 | Keyboard methodology, focus trap/restoration, missing dynamic states |
| 🔍 4 | Task 4 | Screen reader matrix, announcement evidence, mobile AT coverage |
| 🔍 5 | Task 5 | Contrast, reflow, focus appearance, high contrast and reduced motion |
| 🔍 6 | Task 6 | Third-party ownership, VPAT exception language, alternative paths |
| 🔍 7 | Task 7 | Severity consistency, shared-component leverage, remediation priority |
| 🔍 8 | Task 8 | VPAT claim accuracy, evidence sufficiency, overclaiming risk |

---

## Architecture Overview

The audit covers 20 pages, 8 shared components, and 3 third-party integrations using WCAG-EM sampling. The highest-risk architecture areas are authenticated forms, session timeout modal, custom date picker, file upload, search live results, global mega-menu, and third-party CAPTCHA/media.

Primary ARIA/APG patterns:

- Disclosure
- Modal Dialog
- Combobox, if search suggestions are selectable
- Date Picker Dialog
- Sortable Table
- Status / Alert
- Native form controls with field-level error association

Keyboard design emphasis:

- Full Tab path through critical journeys
- Enter/Space activation
- Escape dismissal
- Focus trap and restoration
- Arrow navigation only where APG composite widgets require it

---

## Failure Modes

This plan is designed to prevent:

- Treating all 20 pages equally and missing high-risk application flows.
- Publishing VPAT claims based only on automated scans.
- Treating axe, WAVE, and Lighthouse as interchangeable.
- Missing mobile screen reader testing.
- Ignoring third-party limitations in CAPTCHA and media embeds.
- Ranking findings by WCAG number instead of user impact.
- Fixing symptoms page by page instead of shared components.
- Announcing every field error through repeated assertive live regions.
- Using color-only status, error, or notification indicators.
- Missing rendered DOM verification for ARIA attributes.
- Overclaiming “Supports” in the VPAT when unresolved barriers remain.
